title: pi-context leverage audit: how fully pi-project-workflows dogfoods the tool for its own PM, and the shipped-but-unused vs blocked-on-tracked-gap leverage gaps
question: Which pi-context features and functionalities does pi-project-workflows under-use or not use for managing its own development in .context, and which should it leverage to fully use the tool for its purposes — distinguishing shipped-but-unused (closable by discipline) from blocked-on-a-tracked-capability-gap?
method: code-inspection (ops-registry.ts, SKILL.md, monorepo + package README, CHANGELOG) + config/substrate inspection via the pi-context CLI (read-config block_kinds/relation_types/lenses/invariants, read-block-page per kind, context-current-state) + cross-reference against .context open framework-gaps/features about pi-context capability
scope: the open FGAPs/FEATs about pi-context capability
findings_summary: pi-project-workflows both develops pi-context AND uses pi-context's .context substrate as its own PM system; this audits how fully the project leverages pi-context's capability surface for that PM purpose. Leverage is substantial but uneven. The write, query, and schema-evolution surfaces are driven hard — 93 framework-gaps, 64 tasks, 50 verifications, 17 migrations, live invariant enforcement; the update / resolve-blocked / resolve-conflict arc IS the dev work. But the organizational and derivational surfaces are under-used: ~7 of 17 block kinds sit empty (context-contracts, phase, requirements, rationale, spec-reviews, layer-plans, work-orders) and only ~13 of 40 relation_types carry any edges. Top leverage gaps: (1) No release/version vocabulary — no first-class binding of a version to its completed dev-items + changelog lines, for a lockstep-release monorepo [FGAP-011, accepted]. (2) context-current-state is non-load-bearing — returns 'no active focus' and derives blocked only from task_depends_on_task, ignoring *_gated_by_item gates; the project shadows it with the hand-maintained CLAUDE.md active-arc tracker, violating substrate-as-single-source [FGAP-061 gate-aware derivation + FEAT-004 config-driven derivation]. (3) context-contracts + gather-execution-context sit empty while the orchestrator hand-composes the exact subagent context bundles that op assembles — a shipped capability unused [discipline]. (4) feature/story containment edges, spec-reviews, phases, and roadmaps are shipped-but-empty — the orphan tasks and the unshipped FEAT-001..004 arc have no structural rollup, which is why release-pinning and focus are not derivable [discipline + the derivation gaps]. (5) Warranted non-invariant closure-table edges are systematically under-filed (only requires-edge-invariant-backed relation_types are enforced) [FGAP-091, forcing-function as residual]. Split: ~6 leverage gaps are closable today by filing-discipline alone (populate context-contracts; file feature/story containment edges; backfill warranted edges; use spec-reviews; file phases/roadmap; file session edges); 4 are blocked on a tracked capability landing (FGAP-011 release vocab, FGAP-061 gate-aware derivation, FEAT-004 config-driven derivation, FGAP-091 forcing-function). Critically: no untracked capability gap surfaced — every 'the tool can't do this' is already filed in .context; the remainder is pure dogfooding-discipline debt. Headline implication: the project's inability to derive its own focus / status / release-plan (the recurring operating-context pain) is NOT a missing tool capability but a combination of under-population of shipped organizational vocabulary and the small set of tracked derivation/vocabulary gaps.
stale_conditions: FGAP-011 lands (release/version vocabulary added) — the no-release-vocabulary finding becomes obsolete
FGAP-061 and/or FEAT-004 land (gate-aware / config-driven derivation) — context-current-state becomes load-bearing and the CLAUDE.md shadow tracker retires
FGAP-091's forcing-function invariant lands — the under-filed-edges finding changes
the currently-empty block kinds (context-contracts, spec-reviews, phase, requirements, rationale, work-orders) or the feature/story containment edges become populated
the op surface or the block_kinds/relation_types/lenses/invariants registries change materially from the a38cca9 baseline

citations.label: the full leverage audit (capability surface inventory, current-usage inventory, prioritized leverage gaps, the discipline-vs-tracked-gap split)
gap-task-feature coverage + release-pinnability audit (45 open gaps, sparse coverage, 4 release-pinnable features)
FGAP-091 — no forcing-function for warranted non-invariant edges (identified)
